We all have friends that you know you start with and it's like who gets there first right who gets to the finish line and then There's a lot of our friends behind us And we find a way to try to help them if we can and I remember one of my friends was a was a rider and of course I sent him on a lot of you know jobs and every report that came back was he just pissed everybody off and Finally came over and he goes you know I hate Hollywood. I had all you people and I said you know what I tried to help you man You know and I said some of these people you're talking bad about or actually become friends of mine And and I said you know and I don't understand this whole thing about Hollywood I said maybe maybe yours even though a rider always thinks the last thing they wrote is the very best thing they wrote I said maybe it's just not good enough and he kind of said one more thing to me and it just really Crossed the line and he's a kid from the 60s sort of speaking You know a protest march or anything and it's very easy to put Hollywood down But he crossed the line with a friend of me and I don't know what happened, but I had him up against the wall and I said look I said you know would you just quit putting everybody down? I said you know quit writing things that are 120 pages long because in our vernacular that means a script I said and if you really want to write write something It's 88 pages or 888 and I realized I had my hands on my Sent around I thought probably we had lost our friendship and of course a week later He says hey, I don't have a place to stay could I stay at your house? So he stays there for a couple of months and he's writing every night. He says will you read what I wrote today? I said no And this went on and finally started reading to my daughter who was like three every night and finally my wife said to me She said look he's in his room reading He's got to go and I finally said yeah too much you got to go so away He went he left what he had written and he went down to Arizona And he was working in a Chinese restaurant if you've ever worked in a Chinese restaurant washing dishes. There's a lot of them Call me said have you read it read read what I wrote? I said no I'm not I don't really like you anymore because well, I'm cold. I'm working at this place And I'm having to kill raccoons and work at this Chinese restaurant And and I said So I sent him a sleeping bag I sent him stuff and he said did did you read what I and finally I read it and it was dances with wool no And I when you have that situation somebody that you know you start with I Was never so proud of somebody I said you did it man. You really really did it I said I don't know how I'm gonna do it, but I'm gonna make it into a movie and and that's Was a story of actually a lot of my career so many of those movies Great had to push them up to the point I'm killing raccoons and washing dishes to winning an Oscar was already amazing before the punchline that you know It was I never help people And all new episode of Graham Thursdays at 11 30 get caught up full episodes at BBC America comm on demand in the BBC America mobile app
